parsed_review.summary: CP00-479 (Risk C, 65 units, RP15.P09.M06.S08-RP15.P09.M10.S04) is a clean descriptor-only closeout slice that closes the RP15 Firm Analytics program and hands off to RP16.P00.M00.S01/CP00-480. The pack-level production_ready claim is correctly FALSE (manifest status implemented_pending_review, runtime_ready false, implementation_layer descriptor); local validation only yields production_ready_candidate=true, with final closeout gated behind this Claude review plus human approval. No-write/no-real-data/no-runtime boundaries are intact: freezeCp479Result forces every no-write flag after the result spread, the no-write attestation chain inherits all-false safety flags from the CP453 base, and the boundary attestation (claude_not_final_approver, enterprise_trust_not_claimed_from_local_validation, no_hermes_runtime_receipt, no real data) is consistent across registry/service/validators/contract/manifest/README. Claude remains read-only (allowed_tools Read/Grep/Glob) and explicitly not final approver. Hermes patterns are used only as deterministic descriptor/evidence strings, not product runtime. All deliverable/micro distributions reconcile to 65; row keys are collision-free; the validator deep-equals the contract projection against freshly built descriptors (strong drift protection) and all 7 required public exports resolve through index.js export *. CP178+ review hardening is encoded (forbidden_review_evidence covers auth failure, zero-byte, malformed/fenced JSON, tool-call-shaped output, missing fields, final-approval/enterprise-trust claims), and the auth preflight evidence is valid (exit 0, 1446-byte well-formed JSON). The only observation is a non-blocking harness classification overlap in changed-file-scope.json. No P0/P1/P2 issues; does not block pack or goal closeout.

findings.title: changed-file-scope.json double-classifies the pack's own manifest dir and the session-handoff doc
findings.details: docs/closeout-packs/cp00-479/ (lines 132 and 138) and docs/closeout-pack-plan/new-session-handoff.md (lines 131 and 137) each appear in BOTH untracked_implementation_files and unrelated_dirty_files_preserved. The cp00-479 directory is this pack's own manifest/command-evidence output, so labeling it 'unrelated_dirty_files_preserved' is internally contradictory. This is a review-harness scope-classification artifact only; the authoritative active_pack_implementation_refs list (the 8 analytics files) is correct, and the 7 modified (M) git rows exactly match the active refs, so pack scope determination is unaffected.
findings.recommendation: Have the scope-generation tooling dedupe entries so a path that qualifies as an untracked_implementation_file is not also emitted under unrelated_dirty_files_preserved. No change required to the pack deliverables for closeout.
